name: Visualizing the Sine Function
chatResponse: I'll guide you through the fundamental behavior of a sine wave, explaining its periodic motion and plotting its characteristic curve from $0$ to $2\pi$.

steps.title: Establishing the Domain
steps.content: To graph a standard sine wave, we look at the input $x$ in radians. A full cycle or **period** occurs every $2\pi$ radians (approximately 6.28 units). This represents one full rotation around a unit circle.

steps.title: Mapping the Amplitude
steps.content: The vertical output, $y = \sin(x)$, represents the height of the wave. In its base form, the **amplitude** is 1, meaning the wave oscillates between a maximum of $+1$ and a minimum of $-1$.

steps.title: Identifying Critical Points
steps.content:
The wave follows a predictable path: 
- It starts at the origin $(0, 0)$.
- Reaches a peak at $\pi/2 \approx 1.57$.
- Crosses the x-axis again at $\pi \approx 3.14$.
- Hits a trough at $3\pi/2 \approx 4.71$.
- Returns to zero at $2\pi \approx 6.28$.
